channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Study maps US diabetes–cancer hotspots
item.description: A new University of Kentucky study has mapped areas across the U.S. where high rates of diabetes and deaths from diabetes-related cancers overlap. The...

item.title: Targeting a dangerous gut infection: Studies reveal how C. diff behaves inside the body
item.description: Affecting roughly half a million Americans each year, bacterial infections caused by Clostridioides difficile—commonly known as C. diff—are a serious ...

item.title: First global 'early warning system' for dengue launches
item.description: A new online dashboard will give researchers, governments, and the public a real-time picture of the global dengue situation for the first time. The G...

item.title: Study confirms food fortification is highly cost-effective in fighting hidden hunger across 63 countries
item.description: A comprehensive new systematic review published in The Journal of Nutrition provides the latest evidence that large-scale food fortification is a high...

item.title: Virtual therapy strengthens social skills in autism, Simville study finds
item.description: An increasing number of people worldwide are affected by autism spectrum disorder (ASD); according to studies, one in 44 children is diagnosed with it...

item.title: RHR: The Gut-Brain Connection: How Your Microbiome Affects Your Mental Health
item.description: Depression and anxiety are often treated as chemical imbalances in the brain—but what if the real problem starts in the gut? In this episode of Revolu...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris challenges the conventional focus on sleep duration and makes the case that sleep timing and circadi...

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...
